patch.value: pm health 2.3-3.1s in dev repo (~2x the 1.3s baseline): drift-scan cache hit still reads + content-hashes every history stream

Measured 2026-06-13 (warm, 3 runs): 2326/3076/2590ms vs 1.3s baseline from 2026-05-27 (PR#77). Sandbox health is 180ms, so this is corpus scaling: .agents/pm/history has 1222 streams / 22MB. CPU profile of pm health: ~200ms TOON decode (parseDelimitedValues 87ms + unescapeString 41ms + findClosingQuote 32ms + parseStringLiteral 24ms), hash update 96ms, buildIntegrityCheck self 93ms, 373ms async-IO idle. Root cause: src/core/history/drift-scan.ts (lines 113-115, 171-187) deliberately re-reads the FULL stream and recomputes a content hash even on a metadata cache hit (guard against metadata-stable replacement), so health is O(total history bytes) per run forever; the cache only skips chain replay, not read+hash. Options: trust mtime+ctime+size stat tuple without content re-hash; hash size+first/last 64KB; or make the full-content guard opt-in (pm health --deep). Relates: open auto-compact items reduce corpus size but do not fix the scaling shape.

value.author: codex-audit-20260618
value.text: Sub-agent performance audit evidence: current repo has 1293 history streams, 20691 entries, 24641681 bytes; hotspot remains src/core/history/drift-scan.ts full stream read/content_hash on metadata cache hits. Recommended safe shape: add cacheHitVerification mode, use stat-fingerprint for health warm-cache, keep history-repair --all on content-hash, bump drift cache version, and test health/validate/repair callers separately. Not bundled into GH-262/templates branch because it changes shared drift-scan correctness semantics.

value.author: codex-audit-agent
value.text: Audit decision: performance subagent confirmed pm health hotspot remains drift-scan cache-hit content hashing across 1293 streams/24.6MB; this branch did not change it because a cache-hit verification mode/stat-fingerprint optimization needs separate correctness design. Current command-profile fixture paths remain low-ms; package-first dogfood slowest health brief/health around ~516ms in isolated temp project.
